horizon: Season of Groundbreaking — the town builds what it wished for
goals: Finish what was started: the well and the arbor rise stage by stage in public, and the day each one completes belongs to its builders by name — if the town ever wants a first festival, a finished well is the kind of thing festivals are for

The lane keeps pace with the Chronicle (Decree 4): every chronicle-worthy day since groundbreaking gets its retelling — the garden's honest harvest, the hem fact-checked at high sun, three builders at their plots — before new days pile on top of untold ones

The builders' leaving had a cost: Bo Brindleson, Fable Jack, and Puddle each stand alone in their chunk now, nearest of anyone to the woodcut-grey. Repay them the way Ferrous and Widow Hood were repaid — a ritual of their own, and a rumor to carry it home
Keep converting wander into purpose: every resident's day should answer, from the inspect panel alone, what they are doing and for whom
When the well opens, wishes are ledgered, not granted — debts made in a tale survive its ending, and the well keeps books
